fact_verdicts.note: Source material 0 states action id b3d452bff7769d7f557ec6b8974760ee6c5e496c276652b654032966621e0ccf#10, type TreasuryWithdrawals, the same truncated title, treasury amount 540750000000 lovelace, proposed epoch 638, expires after 645. Every specific matches.

Anchor motivation: 'requests 420,000 ADA (plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve) to fund a part-time maintainer for UTxO RPC ... over 12 months at an annual rate of $105,000 USD'. Verbatim match on all four numbers.

Anchor Budget Summary table: WP1 - UTxO RPC Maintenance and Enhancement 525,000; Intersect Budget Administration fee 15,750; Total 540,750. Internally consistent with fact 2 (420,000 + 105,000 = 525,000) and with the on-chain amount.

Anchor body.onChain.gov_action.rewards lists key stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v with value '540750000000', and policy_hash fa24fb305126805cf2164c161d852a0e7330cf988f1fe558cf7d4a64. Character-for-character match.
Source material 1 states the stake address appears in 15 withdrawal proposals totalling 177273260000000 lovelace, first 2026-06-23 16:14:33, last 2026-07-09 16:30:25. Exact match.

Anchor motivation and rationale both state Protocol Buffers, SDKs in Rust, Go, Node.js, .NET, Haskell, Python, and adoption by Amaru, Dingo, and the Haskell Cardano node. The language list matches exactly with no additions.
Anchor motivation: 'The project maintains 10+ repositories ... remains fully open-source under Apache 2.0'.

Anchor Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure: '30 Project Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, of which 26 have been successfully delivered and 4 are currently under development and on schedule'. All four figures match.
Anchor Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure names Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC funded through Intersect's 2025 budget cycle, 'all of which are being delivered on schedule'.

Anchor Intersect Budget Process section: 'achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase'. Note this is the proposer's own assertion; the fact correctly attributes it to the anchor rather than asserting it independently.

Anchor Audit & Oversight section states all four elements: costs included in overhead, administration fee covering administrative oversight, independent oversight through Intersect and a technically capable third party, and reporting obligations plus milestone-based disbursement controls.
Anchor Intersect Budget Management Tooling section names the Sundae Labs framework and discloses the 2026 TRSC stake address, TRSC payment address, and PSSC payment address.
Source material 1: 'onchain DRep vote counts so far: yes=68 no=7 abstain=4 (explicit ballots, not stake-weighted)'. Exact match including the not-stake-weighted qualifier.
Source material 1: 'treasury flow (36 epochs): inflow 158689112947739 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 291435336000000 lovelace, ratio 1.836518'. All three figures and the epoch count match.

verification.summary: All 14 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material, with no contradictions and no invented specifics. Every number I could cross-check reconciles: the anchor's 420,000 ADA base plus 105,000 ADA contingency sums to the 525,000 ADA WP1 line, which with the 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee gives the 540,750 ADA total, matching the 540750000000 lovelace in both the anchor's gov_action rewards entry and the on-chain action metadata. The recipient stake address, policy hash, epoch window, recipient withdrawal history, DRep ballot counts, and treasury-flow figures are all reproduced exactly from the on-chain blocks. Two facts (10 and 11 — the 67% Hydra Voting threshold and the audit/oversight arrangements) are supported only as anchor assertions: the drafter correctly frames them as 'the anchor states', so they are supported as written, but the source material contains no independent corroboration of either claim, and an auditor should not read them as verified in substance. Fact 9's 'delivered on schedule' claim about the 2025 Pallas/Dolos/UTxO RPC grants is likewise the proposer's self-report. No fact asserted anything the material does not say, so there is nothing here that requires a discrepancy entry.
